computation_inputs.note: Indicative figures from rec file worked example; actual sizing depends on which appreciated asset is contributed (post-liquidity) and on couple's underwriting outcome.

quantified_impact.qualitative_phrasing: Pairs a CRUT (lifetime income, charitable deduction, charity remainder) with a wealth-replacement ILIT (income-tax-free death benefit to family) so the family is not disinherited by the charitable gift; at indicative $5M asset / $5M survivorship UL scale, family receives ~$5M income-tax-free via ILIT and charity receives ~$5M+ remainder, versus ~$2–3M to family after estate tax with no charitable component absent the structure.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: Until post-liquidity asset mix and basis are known, exact CRUT contribution asset is undecided (HIS sale proceeds, brokerage lots, or other appreciated property).
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: Post-transaction balance sheet finalized and charitable budget confirmed.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: Premium and face amount depend on underwritten offer for Marcus + Catherine survivorship UL; both are listed as preferred-rate insurable but no offer obtained.
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: Survivorship UL underwriting completed for both spouses.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: CRUT payout rate (must satisfy 10% remainder test at current §7520 5.00%) and term (lifetime vs term-of-years) not yet selected; affects income, deduction, and remainder.
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: CRUT design finalized with attorney and §7520 rate locked at funding.

blocked_inputs.blocked_reason: Family foundation vs DAF vs direct public charity decision (Marcus's trade scholarships, Catherine's children's hospital) not finalized; affects deduction AGI limits and remainder design.
blocked_inputs.would_unblock_when: Post-liquidity charitable vehicle decision confirmed (foundation hard-rule preference noted).

scenario_range.computation_method: Indicative wealth-replacement ILIT face amount range based on rec-file worked example ($5M midpoint); low/high reflect smaller/larger appreciated-asset contribution scaling, scaled to plausible post-transaction Holloway charitable budget.

action_items.description: Establish CRUT and contribute appreciated asset, coordinated with REC-CHR-003 (CRUT setup, payout rate selection, charitable remainder beneficiary).
sub_steps: Select CRUT payout rate satisfying 10% remainder test at current §7520 5.00%
Identify appreciated asset to contribute (post-liquidity)
Coordinate qualified appraisal if non-publicly-traded asset
Finalize charitable remainder beneficiary (DAF / family foundation / public charity)

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Check in with {{partner_type}} on CRUT drafting and funding milestones for {{rec_id}}

action_items.description: Establish ILIT with institutional trustee, coordinated with REC-EST-004, to own survivorship life policy on Marcus and Catherine.
sub_steps: Engage estate attorney specializing in ILIT + CRUT integration
Designate institutional trustee
Establish Crummey beneficiaries and notice protocol

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Check in with {{partner_type}} on ILIT drafting and trustee onboarding for {{rec_id}}

action_items.description: Underwrite survivorship universal life policy on Marcus and Catherine; secure offer and place coverage owned by ILIT.
sub_steps: Submit applications for both spouses (both reported preferred-rate insurable)
Obtain medical exams and APS
Compare carrier illustrations and select policy
Bind policy with ILIT as owner and beneficiary

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Check in with {{partner_type}} on survivorship UL underwriting status for {{rec_id}}

action_items.description: Implement Crummey notice protocol for annual exclusion gifts funding ILIT premium.
sub_steps: Trustee issues written Crummey notices to each beneficiary on each contribution
Track 30–60 day withdrawal window
Maintain notice + acknowledgment file
Apply hanging-Crummey mechanics if needed to avoid 5/$5,000 lapse-as-gift

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Confirm with {{partner_type}} that annual Crummey notices have been issued and acknowledged for {{rec_id}}

action_items.description: Coordinate annual CRUT 4-tier income reporting, ILIT premium funding, and Crummey administration across CPA, attorney, and trustee.
sub_steps: CPA prepares CRUT 4-tier income reporting (Form 5227 / K-1 to donor)
PSA confirms premium paid and ILIT funded
Trustee maintains administration file

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Confirm with {{partner_type}} that CRUT 4-tier reporting and ILIT premium funding completed for {{rec_id}}

action_items.description: Conduct annual review of CRUT performance, ILIT policy performance, and family/charitable alignment.
sub_steps: Review CRUT investment performance vs payout obligation
Review survivorship UL in-force illustration
Confirm continued charitable intent and beneficiary designations

auto_generated_reminder_template.reminder_text_template: Conduct annual CRUT + ILIT review for {{rec_id}}

recommendations._audit_notes: State B: formula identified but key inputs (specific appreciated asset, UL underwriting offer, CRUT payout rate, charitable vehicle choice) are post-liquidity decisions. Indicative computation uses rec-file worked example at $5M scale. §7520 rate cited at May 2026 = 5.00% (current at funding). Cluster id assigned to bind CRUT+ILIT bailout; sequencing relations (must_come_after REC-CHR-003 and REC-EST-004; coordinated_with REC-RSK-004) copied from batch context and rec file. Insurance broker partner_type set to Other since enum lacks a generic life-insurance broker (Health Insurance Broker is wrong category).

section_assignment_ambiguity.source_metadata_text: Plan section placement: Specialty Charitable / Estate

timing_bucket_inferred.source_signal: Rec file lacks explicit timing; structure depends on post-liquidity asset and charitable budget, placing it in the post-transaction window.
